title: 8 Natural Essential Oils for Older Adults Experiencing Chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contain compounds like eucalyptol and alpha-pinene that have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to ease chills by reducing mucus in the body and relieving congestion in the lungs. When you inhale the oil, the eucalyptol can help to open up airways, making it easier to breathe.  To use the oil to help with chills, you can add a few drops to a diffuser or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief. You can also mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut oil and apply it to your chest or back.  When you use Eucalyptus globulus essential oils to help with chills, you may start to feel relief from congestion and a reduction in body temperature. Your breathing may become easier, and you may feel more comfortable. You may also find that your cough subsides and your energy levels increase.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils, also known as ginger oil, has been traditionally used to combat chills due to its warming properties and active constituents. The oil contains compounds like gingerol, shogaol, and zingerone, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. These properties help to increase blood circulation, relieve pain and stiffness, and promote sweating, all of which can help to alleviate chills. If you're experiencing chills, you can try adding a few drops of ginger oil to a warm bath or inhaling it through steam inhalation. You can also mix the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to your skin, focusing on areas like your chest, neck, and wrists. Alternatively, you can add ginger oil to your diffuser or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief. Taking action with Zingiber officinale essential oils may lead to a reduction in chills and an improvement in your overall well-being. You may start to feel warmer and more comfortable, with a decrease in body aches and pains. The increased circulation and sweating may also help to clear out any underlying infections or toxins, promoting a faster recovery. However, it's essential to note that if your chills are severe or persistent, you should consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils, also known as tea tree oil, can help with chills due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The main constituents of this oil, such as terpinen-4-ol and cineole, help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause fever and chills. When you inhale the oil or apply it to your skin, these compounds can help to calm your body's response to infection, reducing the severity of chills and fever. To use Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils for chills, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to your skin, or inhale it through steam inhalation or a diffuser. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath to help relax your body and reduce fever. When you do this, you may start to feel a reduction in chills and fever, and your body may begin to feel more calm and relaxed. As your body responds to the oil, you may notice a decrease in chills and fever, and you may feel more comfortable and relaxed. Your skin may also appear clearer and healthier, as the oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat any underlying infections. With consistent use, you may be able to reduce the severity and frequency of chills and fever, helping you to feel better and recover faster.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils, also known as rosemary essential oil, is a natural remedy for chills due to its decongestant and immune-boosting properties. It contains constituents like camphor, bornyl acetate, and beta-pinene, which help to thin mucus and ease congestion in the chest and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. When inhaled, rosemary essential oil can also help to reduce inflammation and stimulate the body's natural immune response, fighting off underlying infections that may be causing the chills. To use rosemary essential oil for chills, add a few drops to a diffuser or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief. You can also mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to your chest or back to help ease congestion and soothe your muscles. When using rosemary essential oil for chills, it's likely that you will start to feel relief from congestion and discomfort within a few minutes, and your body's natural temperature will begin to return to normal. As your body's immune response is stimulated, you may also start to feel more energized and alert, and your overall sense of well-being may improve.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils have been traditionally used to help alleviate chills and fever. This is due to the presence of active compounds such as alkylamides, glycosides, and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The main constituents, including echinacoside and cichoric acid, work together to boost the immune system and fight off infections. When you apply Echinacea purpurea essential oils topically, specifically to the feet or wrists, they are absorbed into the bloodstream and can help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms.  To use Echinacea purpurea essential oils for chills, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and apply it to the affected areas. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath or inhale the oil through steam inhalation to help ease congestion and promote relaxation. It's recommended to use high-quality, pure Echinacea purpurea essential oils and consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're pregnant or have any underlying health conditions.  Taking action with Echinacea purpurea essential oils may lead to a reduction in chills and fever, as well as a decrease in body aches and pains. You may also experience improved sleep quality and a sense of overall well-being. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea essential oils are not a substitute for medical treatment and should be used in conjunction with conventional care.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils, also known as thyme oil, can help with chills due to its antimicrobial and warming properties. Thyme oil contains constituents like thymol, carvacrol, and linalool, which have antiviral and antifungal properties that can help combat infections that cause chills. When you inhale thyme oil, its compounds can stimulate the brain's thermoregulation center, which helps increase your body temperature. To use thyme oil for chills, you can add a few drops to your diffuser or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief. You can also mix a few drops of thyme oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to your skin, focusing on areas like your chest, back, and arms. Another option is to add thyme oil to your bath water for a warm and relaxing soak. When you inhale or apply thyme oil topically, you may start to feel a warming sensation in your body, which can help reduce chills. You may also experience a sense of relief from congestion and coughing, as thyme oil has expectorant properties that can help loosen mucus. As your body temperature increases and your congestion clears, you may start to feel more energetic and comfortable, which can help you recover from chills faster.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils, also known as licorice root oil, may help alleviate chills due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The oil contains constituents like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the body's immune response. This can be beneficial in reducing the severity of chills caused by viral infections like the common cold or flu. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils for chills, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply topically to the chest or back. You can also inhale the oil by adding a few drops to a diffuser or by directly inhaling the oil from a cloth or handkerchief. When inhaled, the oil may help ease congestion and reduce the feeling of chilliness. As you take action, you may start to feel a reduction in your body temperature and an improvement in your overall well-being. The antiviral properties of the oil may also help to shorten the duration of your illness, allowing you to recover faster. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce any associated symptoms like body aches or headaches. However, it's essential to note that Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment, especially if you have a severe or persistent illness. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using essential oils, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or have any underlying health conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils, also known as lemongrass oil, can help alleviate chills due to its decongestant and antiseptic properties. The main constituents of lemongrass oil, such as citral, geranial, and neral, have a warming effect on the body, which helps to reduce shivering and fever. When inhaled, the oil's citrusy scent can help open up airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. To use lemongrass oil for chills, you can add a few drops to a diffuser, inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief, or mix it with a carrier oil for a warm compress. Apply the compress to your chest or forehead to help loosen mucus and reduce body temperature. As you inhale the oil's vapors or apply the compress, you may start to feel warmer and more comfortable, with your shivers subsiding. The oil's antiseptic properties can also help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to your chills, promoting overall well-being and helping your body to recover faster.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for chills

intro_desc: As people age, their bodies go through natural changes that can affect their ability to regulate temperature. Older adults may experience chills due to a decrease in muscle mass, a reduction in the body's ability to produce heat, or underlying health conditions such as anemia or hypothyroidism. When older adults get chills, it can be a sign of a more serious issue, and it can also be uncomfortable and disrupt their daily lives. Chills can make it difficult to sleep, cause fatigue, and even lead to falls or other accidents. Older adults may also experience anxiety or depression due to the discomfort and uncertainty of not knowing what's causing their chills.  Chills can be especially problematic for older adults who may already have difficulty managing their daily needs, such as bathing, dressing, or taking medication. When they're feeling chilled, it can be hard for them to get out of bed, get dressed, or participate in activities they enjoy. This can lead to feelings of isolation and frustration. In some cases, chills can be a symptom of an underlying infection, such as pneumonia or urinary tract infection, which requires medical attention.  Herbal essential oils can be a helpful addition to the treatment of chills in older adults. Certain oils have natural warming properties that can help increase circulation and relax the body, making it easier to manage chills. These oils can be inhaled, applied topically, or added to a warm bath, providing relief from discomfort and promoting relaxation.
